source.title: Crane Lifting Pin Failure Incident - Vermilion Block 38M

context.region: Gulf of America, Vermilion Block 38, Lease 00204

actions_taken: IP placed in personnel basket and lifted to liftboat
IP transported to Lafayette General Hospital at 6:00 p.m.
Crane main hoist line inspected on July 13, 2025 and put out of service
Main hoist line replaced on July 20, 2025
Independent third party pull testing conducted July 23, 2025
Casing jacks used to remove stuck casing on July 29, 2025
event.summary: On July 12, 2025, at approximately 3:30 p.m., a casing lifting pin failed during well abandonment operations on the VR-38M platform. The 2-inch diameter lifting pin bent into a U-shape under excessive load and struck a Supreme Services employee in the face, causing severe injuries including subdural hematoma, skull fracture, and facial bone fractures. The incident occurred when the crane operator attempted to lift stuck 7-inch casing using the main hoist. Investigation revealed the casing became stuck because a spool riser with 11.00-inch inside diameter was installed over a seal ring with 11.44-inch outside diameter. The lifting pin failed at an estimated load exceeding 102,000 pounds, well above its 60,000-pound safe working load limit. Contributing factors included improper sling installation with D-rings not adjacent to the casing, exceeding the approved lift plan's maximum weight capacity of 24,255 pounds, and failure to call an all-stop when the casing became stuck.
recommendations: BSEE Lake Charles District recommends Office of Incident Investigations issue a Safety Alert/Bulletin regarding this incident

hazards.name: Mechanical Energy from Crane Lifting Operations

threats.name: Dimensional incompatibility between spool riser and seal ring
Exceeding safe working load limit of lifting equipment
Improper rigging installation (D-rings not adjacent to casing)
Failure to stop operations when casing became stuck
Use of incorrect hoist (main vs auxiliary) without plan revision

consequences.name: Personnel injury - severe facial and skull trauma
Equipment damage - crane main hoist wire rope
Equipment damage - casing lifting pin destroyed

controls.name: Crane Lifting Plan approval by Ultimate Work Authority

supporting_text: The plan was approved by the UWA on July 12, 2025, using the main hoist line, at 12:00 p.m.
the Max Weight Capacity to be pulled could not exceed 24,255 pounds
the operator exceeded the plan's maximum weight capacity of 24,255 pounds

controls.name: Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA) for crane operations

supporting_text: Helix personnel completed and signed a Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA) for the crane operations
The Helix crane operations JSEA and Supreme's pulling casing with crane BJSEA did not mention the maximum lifting weight capacity of 24,255 pounds

controls.name: Behavioral Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (BJSEA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify behavioral hazards in crane operations

supporting_text: Supreme personnel completed and signed a Behavioral Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (BJSEA) for crane operations
The Helix crane operations JSEA and Supreme's pulling casing with crane BJSEA did not mention the maximum lifting weight capacity of 24,255 pounds

controls.name: NNW procedure for casing cut and retrieve (NNW-PRO-SOP.140)
controls.barrier_role: Provide safe work instructions for casing operations

supporting_text: NNW-PRO-SOP.140 Single String Casing Cut and Retrieve, was written for a drilling rig utilizing a Top Drive with Bails, not a crane on a liftboat

the procedure incorrectly states that the lifting capacity for 2-inch casing pins is 67,000 pounds; whereas, a certified two-inch pin pull analysis dated October 25, 2019, provided by NNW, states the safe working load limit is 60,000 pounds
In the certified pull pin analysis, there is a note that states, 'the pull linkages (D-rings) are required to be adjacent to the caisson in order to minimize bending load on the pin'

controls.name: Certified casing lifting pin with 60,000 lb safe working load
controls.barrier_role: Provide adequate lifting capacity for casing operations

supporting_text: OII investigators inspected the failed casing lifting pin with a safe working load limit of 60,000 pounds
The lifting pin was bent into a U-shape and had gouges where it contacted the 7-inch casing
The casing pin failed at 102,500 pounds
the operator exceeded the manufacturer's safe working load limit of the casing lifting pin of 60,000 pounds
The sling is rated for 52,000 pounds at a length of 4 feet with a cable diameter of 1.25 inches and D-rings installed in the thimble eye
The sling was recertified on November 4, 2024, and showed signs of wear including small separations in the strands
The two-part sling was not installed correctly onto the casing lifting pin. The D-rings were not placed directly adjacent to the casing

The pictures taken by OII at the NNW yard at the NNW shop in Carrier, Mississippi, show markings on the pin indicating the D-rings were not adjacent to the casing. It was estimated that the D-rings were approximately two inches from the casing

controls.name: Crane load monitoring system with audible alarms
controls.barrier_role: Alert operator when maximum lifting capacity reached

supporting_text: the auxiliary hoist and main hoist lines are monitored by an onboard display
both hoists have audible alarms that sound when reaching the maximum lifting point, which is determined by the crane's radius and boom angle
When the maximum weight limit is reached, the hoists are locked out from lifting a heavier weight
the weight indicator had an approximate one second delay from the sensor to the onboard display
They installed a spool riser onto the wellhead, which had an inside diameter of 11.00 inches, on top of the exposed seal ring, which had a diameter of 11.44 inches
the seal ring, which was approximately .44 inches larger than the 11-inch 3M by 11-inch 5M riser spool, could not drift through the spool riser at the time of the incident

the operator installed a spool riser with an inside diameter of 11.00 inches above a wellhead seal ring with an outside diameter of 11.44 inches. This dimensional incompatibility caused the seal ring to become stuck inside the spool riser

controls.name: All-stop procedure when abnormal conditions encountered
controls.barrier_role: Stop operations when unexpected resistance encountered

supporting_text: An all-stop was also not called to address the stuck casing
The crane lifted the casing until it became stuck in the well
Casing pipe got stuck in hole while using the AUX line block! So, I was ask to bring down big blk to hook up slings and pull casing pipe up

controls.name: Crane operator competency and certification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure qualified personnel operate crane safely

supporting_text: the field for the name of the crane operator was left blank
the crane operator was listed as the supervisor of the lift
The crane operator attempted to lift the second section of stuck casing with the main hoist, which was not free to be lifted
The crane operated as designed and no failures were noted that contributed to the incident

on July 20, 2025, an S&A Services crane technician at the Helix yard in Fourchon, Louisiana, replaced the damaged main hoist line on the liftboat Dallas's crane. The technician also completed a quarterly inspection

controls.name: Medic on-site for emergency response
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical care following injury

supporting_text: The medic was called to scene immediately following the incident
The medic evaluated the IP and prepared him to be moved
While waiting on the helicopter, the IP was moved to the liftboat TV room for observation and first aid

controls.name: Medevac helicopter emergency evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Rapidly transport injured personnel to hospital

supporting_text: A medevac helicopter was called at 3:36 p.m.
The medevac helicopter landed on the liftboat at 5:45 p.m. and departed with the IP for Lafayette General Hospital at 6:00 p.m.

controls.name: Personnel basket for safe transfer of injured worker
controls.barrier_role: Safely move injured personnel between platform and liftboat

supporting_text: The IP was then placed in a personnel basket to be lifted from the platform to the liftboat

controls.name: Crane post-incident inspection
controls.barrier_role: Detect equipment damage after shock loading

supporting_text: on July 13, 2025, the liftboat Dallas's crane was inspected due to being shock loaded during the incident
The crane technician inspected the main hoist line and stated that he 'found 1 strand damaged and 3 other strands with damaged wires'
The main hoist line was put out of service

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
